title: Heatwave triggers 20% rise in stomach issues in Karnataka, doctors link it to dehydration and food contamination - NewsFirst Prime
full_text:
Extreme summer conditions lead to dehydration, infections, and indigestion; health concerns grow as people report more stomach pain and fatigue.
The intense heatwave sweeping across Karnataka is not just making people uncomfortable but is also affecting their health. Doctors are now reporting a noticeable increase in stomach-related problems, with cases rising by nearly 20 percent due to the extreme summer conditions.
With temperatures climbing sharply, many people are experiencing dehydration, fatigue, and digestive issues. Hospitals are seeing a growing number of patients complaining of stomach pain, discomfort, and weakness, especially during peak daytime heat.
Health experts say one of the main reasons behind this rise is dehydration. Due to excessive heat, the body loses a large amount of water, which directly affects digestion. When the body lacks sufficient fluids, the digestive system slows down, often leading to stomach pain.
Another major concern is food spoilage. In hot weather, food gets spoiled much faster. Consuming such contaminated or stale food can lead to stomach infections and severe discomfort. Along with this, bacteria and viruses tend to multiply quickly in high temperatures, increasing the chances of infections related to the stomach.
Contaminated drinking water is also adding to the problem. During summer, water sources can get polluted easily, and consuming unsafe water can cause multiple digestive issues.
Experts have also pointed out that excessive intake of cold items like ice creams and chilled drinks can disturb the stomach. Though these may offer temporary relief from heat, they can lead to irritation and pain in the digestive system.

Indigestion is another common issue during this season. The body’s digestion process slows down in extreme heat, leading to gas, bloating, and stomach pain. Irregular eating habits and increased consumption of outside or street food further worsen the situation.
Doctors advise people to stay cautious during the summer months by maintaining proper hydration, eating fresh food, and avoiding unhealthy dietary habits. With the heatwave expected to continue, health officials warn that awareness and preventive care are essential to avoid further complications.
